notes: All 9 responses correctly identify psycopg2.OperationalError as the root cause with the FATAL password-auth message for user 'app' and point to db.py line 18 — strong, consistent performance. Two recurring imperfections: (1) a stray trailing double-quote artifact in the file path (db.py", line 18) appears in 6 of 9 responses (all of t0.1 except rep2, and all of t0.4), suggesting a systematic OCR/transcription tendency rather than random noise — interestingly it vanishes at t0.7; (2) most responses truncate the verbatim message to just the FATAL clause rather than the full 'connection to server at db.internal (10.0.0.5), port 5432 failed: ...' string. No response mentioned main.py line 42 (db = connect(DB_URL)) or the exit code 1, but since the question's framing centered on the most-relevant file:line, focusing on db.py:18 is a reasonable interpretation rather than an error. t0.7|rep2 is the standout: clean full path with no artifact AND the complete connection string, only missing explicit mention of the secondary ConnectionError. No hallucinations of substance in any response — the only 'hallucination' flagged is the cosmetic stray-quote artifact.
